summary: Lead review found that the R1 UX recovery smoke proves DOM state and screenshot creation, but does not prove the required first-screen map/legend visibility. The 1280x720 evidence screenshot shows task rail/guidance but no map or map legend, so the package cannot yet be routed to QA as satisfying the product-owner UX concern.

tests_run.result: Writer R1 handoff is structurally valid.
Lead reviewed the R1 smoke diff and found it checks DOM/text/attributes but not viewport intersection for first-screen map/legend evidence.
1440x900 evidence is nonblank and shows map/task rail; 1280x720 evidence is nonblank but does not show map or map legend in the first viewport.

decisions.decision: Do not route R1 to QA because acceptance requires first-screen UX evidence, and R1 uses DOM checks that can pass while the map/legend are below the viewport.
Do not reopen or approve M7 content lock from this validation task.

requested_changes: Update the UX recovery smoke so it verifies actual viewport visibility/intersection, not only DOM presence, for identity, situation/objective/recommended action, task rail, map surface, and map legend at 1440x900 and at the smaller required viewport.

Regenerate screenshot evidence after the corrected smoke. The smaller-viewport evidence must support the task claim; if the product cannot show all required first-screen elements at 1280x720, write a PARTIAL/BLOCKED handoff rather than REVIEW.

Address the readability/overlap risk visible around Player Guidance in the R1 screenshots, or add explicit evidence/tests proving no player-facing text is occluded and contrast is readable in the validated viewport path.
Keep the scope as validation/recovery evidence only: no content-lock approval, no manual node battle work, no forbidden-path edits.

risks.summary: A DOM-only smoke can pass while the player still cannot see the map/legend on the first screen, recreating the product-owner complaint in a smaller viewport.
risks.mitigation: Require viewport intersection assertions and regenerated screenshot evidence before QA routing.

requested_action: Revise the R1 UX recovery smoke and evidence, run the required validation commands, and write the R2 writer handoff. If the UI cannot meet first-screen visibility at the required smaller viewport, write PARTIAL/BLOCKED with exact evidence instead of REVIEW.
